name: Sherried Baked Duck
recipeInstructions:
1. Place sherry in a heavy medium saucepan; heat just until warm, but do not boil.
2. Remove from heat and let stand until flames disappear.
3. Add the next 7 ingredients; stir well to combine.
4. Season ducks with salt and place 1 bay leaf and an onion quarter in the cavity of each duck.
5. Arrange ducks breast side down in a large roaster.
6. Pour half of the hot sherry mixture over the ducks; cover and bake at 350°F for 3 hours, or until tender, basting occasionally with pan drippings.
7. Reheat the remaining sherry mixture, then pour it over the ducks. Let stand covered in the refrigerator for 1 to 2 hours, or until ready to reheat and serve if desired.
8. Cut the ducks in half and return them to the roaster. Cover and bake at 350°F for an additional 30 minutes, or until hot. Place the ducks on a serving platter and garnish with orange slices and fresh parsley sprigs if desired.
